senses.definition: A treatment intended to make the heart muscle grow or work better, often by using substances that support heart tissue growth.
examples: Cardiotrophotherapy is discussed as a possible heart-repair approach in some research contexts.
The goal of cardiotrophotherapy is improved heart function and recovery.

senses.definition: Relating to the heart and blood vessels, or to the whole circulatory system.
examples: Cardiovascular health depends on how well your heart and blood vessels work.
The doctor checked my cardiovascular system.

senses.definition: A medical procedure that stops an abnormal heart rhythm and restores a normal rhythm.
examples: Cardioversion can bring an irregular heartbeat back to normal.
The cardiologist recommended cardioversion after tests.

senses.definition: Plural of cardioversion; procedures that restore a normal heart rhythm from an abnormal one.
examples: Several cardioversions were performed to control the rhythm.
The clinic keeps records of past cardioversions.

senses.definition: Relating to the heart and internal organs, especially in ways the heart and organs work together through body systems.
examples: The report described a cardiovisceral effect.
Researchers studied how cardiovisceral changes appear in the body.

senses.definition: A rare term used in medical contexts to refer to a type of paludism (malaria) associated with the heart or cardiac symptoms.
examples: The unusual case note mentioned cardipaludism.
Cardipaludism was considered during the differential diagnosis.

senses.definition: Inflammation involving both the heart (cardio-) and the lining around the heart (pericardium).
examples: Cardipericarditis can cause chest pain and breathing discomfort.
The doctor looked for signs of cardipericarditis.

senses.definition: A genus name used in biology/medicine contexts (exact meaning depends on the specific scientific usage).
examples: The study referenced the genus cardis in its classification section.
In taxonomy, cardis may refer to a specific group.

senses.definition: Describing something that uses clever reasoning in a way that is intended to mislead, especially in matters connected with the heart or medicine.
examples: The author called the argument cardisophistical.
Her critique said the claim was cardisophistical rather than scientific.

senses.definition: Inflammation of the heart, used in medical naming (for example, when part of a longer diagnosis term).
examples: Cardita can be part of a longer diagnosis that describes heart inflammation.
The report included cardita among possible heart conditions.

senses.definition: Relating to inflammation of the heart.
examples: The patient had carditic involvement.
Carditic symptoms can include chest pain and fatigue.

senses.definition: Inflammation of the heart.
examples: Carditis may occur after certain infections.
The tests suggested carditis.

senses.definition: To cause inflammation of the heart (rare/technical or nonstandard usage).
examples: The hypothesis carditises the heart after exposure to the agent (technical context).
Older texts sometimes use forms like carditises in medical descriptions.

senses.definition: A term used in biology/medicine as part of word formation meaning “heart.”
examples: In medical word roots, cardium often signals a connection to the heart.
Cardium appears in technical terms related to cardiac structures.

senses.definition: Resembling a card in shape or style, especially in a flat, rectangular form.
examples: The package was cardlike and easy to carry.
The material felt cardlike rather than plastic.

senses.definition: A person or business that makes greeting cards or printed cards.
examples: My aunt is a cardmaker and designs her own birthday cards.
We bought our invitations from a cardmaker.

senses.definition: The activity of making handmade greeting cards or decorative cards.
examples: Cardmaking is a relaxing hobby for many people.
She started cardmaking during the holidays.

senses.definition: A plant name; cardo is used for a plant species, depending on the region and language.
examples: In the garden guide, cardo is listed as a local plant.
The herbal description mentions cardo as an ingredient.

senses.definition: A chemical substance name used in chemistry (often related to resins/oils), depending on context.
examples: The lab report identified cardol in the sample.
Cardol is discussed as part of certain plant-derived compounds.

senses.definition: A plant/food-related term used in certain languages or regions; meaning depends on the context (for example, a specific edible plant).
examples: The recipe calls for cardon, a local vegetable.
In the region, cardon is cooked with olive oil.

senses.definition: A family name and place name used in Spanish contexts; it can refer to people or locations named Cardona.
examples: Cardona is the surname of the author.
We visited Cardona, a town with historic buildings.

senses.definition: A plant name used in Spanish contexts; the exact species can vary by region.
examples: The herbal book lists cardoncillo among desert plants.
In local markets, cardoncillo is sold as an herb.

senses.definition: Plural form of cardon; it refers to multiple cardon plants/vegetables in regional usage.
examples: The stew included cardons and potatoes.
Cardons were harvested early this season.

senses.definition: A person who cardoons (rare/technical or nonstandard term); exact meaning is unclear without a specific context.
examples: The text describes a cardooer as someone who prepares the plant.
I couldn’t identify what cardooer means in this passage.

senses.definition: A plant (a type of thistle) with edible parts, often used as a vegetable in Mediterranean cooking.
examples: Cardoon is popular in Italian and Spanish dishes.
We tried a cardoon salad with lemon and olive oil.
